name: Almanza and Gradefes Circular from Cist
name_original: Almanza y Gradefes – circular desde Cistierna
description: This difficult 40.1-mile touring cycling route from Cistierna explores the diverse landscapes and historic villages of Almanza and Gradefes.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cistierna, located in the province of León, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Picos de Europa mountain range, featuring challenging climbs and sculpted valleys. River valleys, such as those carved by the Esla, provide scenic routes, while extensive oak and beech forests contribute to a rich natural environment. The terrain includes both demanding mountain passes and serene valley rides. Best touring cycling routes around Cistierna The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Regional de la Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre – circular from Crémenes, a 42.1 miles (67.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the mountainous terrain of the regional park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Almanza and Gradefes Circular from Cist, a difficult 40.1 miles (64.5 km) path. This route offers a journey through varied landscapes, connecting different towns and showcasing the region's diverse scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Cistierna – El Espinadal loop from Cistierna, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail leading through local countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Cistierna is defined by mountain ranges like the Picos de Europa, river valleys, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging ascents to more moderate rides. The routes in Cistierna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cistierna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cistierna?
A: Cistierna offers a selection of over 20 touring cycling routes, catering to various preferences. These routes are predominantly classified as difficult, with 14 challenging options and 7 moderate ones, ensuring a suitable ride for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cistierna?
A: The terrain around Cistierna is diverse, ranging from challenging mountain passes within the Picos de Europa to serene river valleys, such as those carved by the Esla. You'll encounter significant elevation gains on many routes, with extensive oak and beech forests providing a rich natural environment. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031760435/parque-regional-de-la-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre-circular-from-cremenes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Regional de la Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre – circular from Crémenes&lt;/a&gt; features over 1300 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Cistierna area?
A: Yes, Cistierna offers several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031663016/almanza-and-gradefes-circular-from-cist" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanza and Gradefes Circular from Cist&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 64.5 km path showcasing varied landscapes. Another local favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20499811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cistierna – El Espinadal loop from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt;, a 37.2 km difficult trail through the local countryside.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural beauty and points of interest. You can cycle past the spectacular scenery of the Riaño Reservoir, often called the 'Leonese Fjords,' and enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/306845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilbo Peak&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5475673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Mercadillo&lt;/a&gt;. The region's mining heritage is also visible, with sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina Imponderable&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature significant mountain passes?
A: Absolutely. Cistierna is close to the Picos de Europa, offering routes with challenging mountain passes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mercadillo Bridge – Monte Cotado Pass loop from Fuentes de Peñacorada&lt;/a&gt; includes the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Cotado Pass&lt;/a&gt;. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarna Pass – Maraña Hostel loop from Crémenes&lt;/a&gt;, takes you over the scenic Tarna Pass.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cistierna?
A: The best season for touring cycling in Cistierna is generally spring through autumn. During these months, the weather is more favorable, and the natural landscapes, including the extensive oak and beech forests, are at their most vibrant. Roads in the Picos de Europa are often in excellent condition, making for a pleasant experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cistierna?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cistierna are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 5.0 stars from multiple reviews. Over 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the diverse terrain, from challenging ascents to more moderate rides, and the stunning natural beauty of the region.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes starting from Cistierna?
A: While the guide focuses on routes around Cistierna, the region is known as a starting point for longer journeys. Historically, routes such as 'Cistierna-Bilbao' (261.4 km) and multi-day loops through the Picos de Europa (around 220 km with 6,500 meters of ascent) have been undertaken, showcasing the area's potential for extended touring adventures.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes in Cistierna?
A: Cistierna and its surrounding towns offer various amenities for cyclists. While specific cafes, pubs, or accommodations aren't detailed for each route, the region is accustomed to outdoor enthusiasts. You'll find options in Cistierna itself and in villages along the routes, especially those that serve as popular starting or ending points for tours.

Q: Is Cistierna suitable for cyclists looking for challenging routes?
A: Yes, Cistierna is an excellent destination for cyclists seeking a challenge. The region's proximity to the Picos de Europa means many routes feature significant climbs and descents. Out of the 21 touring cycling routes listed, 14 are classified as difficult, offering ample opportunities for demanding rides and high elevation gains.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Cistierna?
A: Yes, the mountainous landscape of Cistierna provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking viewpoints. Beyond the general &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, you can find specific spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Carande and Las Pintas Massif&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding peaks and valleys.
tips: Charming town with an unmistakable medieval character, its fairytale castle witnessed movie battles and its walls and entrance arch also help to set us in another era. However, not everything is medieval here and proof of this is its unique 28 m tower built in 1947 with the help of all the residents of the town.
The famous Mercadillo bridge is part of a secondary route of the Camino De Santiago and the Lebaniego. This building already existed in the Middle Ages.

This is a town with a strong mining history that can truly claim to be at the foot of the mountains. In 1917, the Republic of Cistierna was declared here for 48 hours, which despite its brief existence had time to position itself against Germany and its allies and in favor of France, Russia and the United Kingdom.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Almanza y Gradefes circular route?
faqs.answer: This route primarily features paved surfaces, but be prepared for some unpaved and challenging sections where you might need to dismount and push your touring bicycle. Near Cistierna and Gradefes, parts of the trail follow the Esla river valley, which includes both asphalt and dirt tracks.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass through the charming medieval town of Almanza with its 'fairytale castle', and cross the historic Mercadillo Bridge, part of the Camino de Santiago. The trail also winds through the scenic Esla river valley and several ancient villages, including Sorriba del Esla with its unique circular dovecote. A significant cultural highlight is the magnificent Romanesque church of the Monastery of Santa María de Gradefes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The Almanza y Gradefes circular route is approximately 64.5 km long and typically takes around 3 hours and 53 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle. This duration assumes a very good fitness level due to the route's 'difficult' classification and significant elevation gain.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail that starts and ends in Cistierna. You can find parking options within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires a very good fitness level due to its length, elevation gain of over 510 meters, and some challenging unpaved sections. It is not recommended for beginners or young families.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While the specific best season isn't detailed, given the varied terrain and potential for unpaved sections, it's advisable to cycle during periods of good weather to avoid muddy conditions or extreme heat. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling in this region.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route overlaps with portions of several historic paths, including the Camino Vadiniense and the Camino Olvidado (Forgotten Way). You'll also find sections that are part of the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, offering glimpses into ancient pilgrimage and mining routes.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Almanza y Gradefes circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through various villages and natural areas. While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through settlements, near livestock, or on shared paths, to ensure safety and respect for local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Almanza y Gradefes circular route. It is a public route accessible to touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or find accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages, including Cistierna (the start/end point), Almanza, and Gradefes. These towns and villages are likely to offer options for cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation, allowing you to refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse landscape, transitioning from mountain foothills to the lush, shaded Esla river valley. The valley provides a 'green pleasure of shade' with varied vegetation, including poplars and agricultural fields. You'll also enjoy views of charming medieval towns and ancient villages with their unique architecture.
